hero.title: Regex Tester
hero.tagline: Paste text below, enter a search pattern, and see live match highlights instantly — no sign-up required. Toggle Regex for full JavaScript regex syntax with capture groups, or leave it off for plain-text search.
hero.blogLink: Learn how to use the Regex Tester →

whatIs.heading: What is Regex
whatIs.body: A regular expression (regex) is a pattern that specifies a set of strings. In JavaScript, regexes are objects (RegExp) that can be applied to strings to test for matches, extract matches, or perform substitutions.

whenToUse.heading: When to use Regex
whenToUse.body: Regex is appropriate when you need to find, validate, or transform text based on a structural pattern rather than a fixed string — for example, validating an email address format, extracting numbers from logs, or replacing all occurrences of a pattern.

flags.heading: Regex Flags
flags.g: g — global: always active. All non-overlapping matches are returned (not just the first).
flags.i: i — case-insensitive: a matches A, B matches b.
flags.m: m — multiline: ^ and $ match start/end of each line, not just the whole string.

captureGroups.heading: Capture Groups & Replacements
captureGroups.body: Parentheses in a regex pattern create capture groups: (\w+). In a replacement string, $1 refers to the first group, $2 to the second, and so on.

usefulWhen.heading: Useful when
usefulWhen.item1: Validating user input against a required format (email, phone, URL).
usefulWhen.item2: Extracting structured fields (dates, IDs, tokens) from unstructured text.
usefulWhen.item3: Performing batch find-and-replace on text with complex rules.

faq.heading: FAQs
faq.q1: Q: What regex flavor does this tester use?
faq.a1: A: JavaScript (ECMAScript) regex, as executed by the browser's built-in RegExp engine.
faq.q2: Q: Why does the match count include overlapping matches?
faq.a2: A: It doesn't — the global flag returns non-overlapping matches in left-to-right order.
faq.q3: Q: Why doesn't my pattern match across lines?
faq.a3: A: Enable the m flag. By default, ^ and $ only match the start and end of the whole string.

howItWorks.step1: Paste or type the text you want to search in the Input Text panel on the left.
howItWorks.step2: Type a search pattern in the Search field. Toggle Regex to use JavaScript regex syntax, or leave it off for a plain-text search.
howItWorks.step3: Matches are highlighted live in the Match Preview panel on the right. The match count updates automatically.
howItWorks.step4: Enter a replacement in the Replace field and click Replace All to see the result. Copy it from the output section that appears below.

howToUse.heading: How to use
howToUse.step1: 1. Paste your text in the Input Text panel.
howToUse.step2: 2. Type a pattern in the Search field.
howToUse.step3: 3. Matches highlight live in the Match Preview panel.
howToUse.step4: 4. Use Replace All to replace matches.

input.label: Input Text
input.placeholder:
Paste or type text here…

Example:
Hello World 123
foo@bar.com

guide.title: How to Test and Debug Regex Quickly (Without Losing Your Mind)
guide.intro: Regular expressions are powerful… but notoriously frustrating. You write a pattern expecting it to match perfectly — and instead it matches too much, nothing, or partially works.
guide.cta: 👉 The key to mastering regex isn't memorization — it's testing and iteration.

learnItems.item3: 👁 Understand what your pattern is actually doing

whatIs.heading: 🔍 What Is a Regex Tester?
whatIs.body: A regex tester is an interactive tool that lets you write a pattern, provide sample text, and instantly see matches — turning regex from guessing into visual feedback.

whyDifficult.heading: 🧠 Why Regex Feels Difficult
whyDifficult.item1: 😵 Compact — a small pattern can represent a lot of logic and be hard to read.
whyDifficult.item2: 🔄 Sensitive — one character change can break everything or completely change behavior.
whyDifficult.item3: 🧩 Abstract — patterns don't always "look like" what they match.

whyUse.heading: ⚡ Why You Should Always Use a Regex Tester
whyUse.item1: 👀 Instant Feedback — see matches as you type; no guessing, no running code repeatedly.
whyUse.item2: 🐞 Faster Debugging — quickly identify wrong groups, missing escapes, incorrect boundaries.
whyUse.item3: 🎯 Better Accuracy — test against real input data and edge cases.
whyUse.item4: 🚀 Faster Learning — experimentation helps you understand patterns and remember syntax naturally.

example.heading: 🧪 Example: Regex in Action
example.result: A regex tester highlights ✅ valid matches and ❌ invalid ones — making debugging much easier.

bestPractices.heading: 🧠 Best Practices for Writing Regex
bestPractices.item1: ✅ Start simple — build patterns step by step, add complexity gradually.
bestPractices.item2: ✅ Test real data — use actual user input and real-world examples.
bestPractices.item3: ✅ Use comments when possible — break complex regex into understandable parts.
bestPractices.item4: ✅ Avoid over-optimization — readable regex beats "clever" regex.
bestPractices.item5: ✅ Validate edge cases — test empty input and unexpected formats.

useCases.heading: 🧑‍💻 Real-World Use Cases
useCases.item1: 📧 Email Validation — check input format before submission
useCases.item3: 📄 Data Extraction — extract IDs, URLs, numbers from text
useCases.item4: 📊 Log Parsing — analyze and filter system logs

proTips.heading: 🚀 Pro Tips
proTips.item1: 🔍 Test small parts of regex first
proTips.item4: 📋 Keep sample inputs saved for reuse

safety.heading: 🔐 Is It Safe to Use a Regex Tester?
safety.warning: 👉 Still avoid pasting sensitive data or production secrets.

faq.heading: ❓ FAQ
faq.q1: Why is my regex not matching anything?
faq.a1: Possible reasons: missing anchors, incorrect syntax, or wrong test input.
faq.q2: Why does my regex match too much?
faq.a2: Likely due to greedy patterns like .*. Try .*? instead.
faq.q3: Can I learn regex without memorizing everything?
faq.a3: Yes — practice with testing tools is the fastest way.
faq.q4: What's the best way to improve regex skills?

guide.conclusion: Regex doesn't have to be frustrating. With the right approach and a good tester, you can build patterns faster, debug with confidence, and truly understand what your regex is doing.
guide.ctaBtn: 👉 Try your regex here: Regex Tester Tool
